text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 60-year-old female with history of FIGO Stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, BRCA1 germline mutation carrier. Status post primary debulking surgery (total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, splenectomy, cholecystectomy, rectosigmoid resection with reanastomosis). Completed 6 cycles carbo/pacli. Currently on nira maintenance therapy, approximately 27 months. Surveillance restaging.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of intravenous iohexol contrast. Images acquired in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformations generated and reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT dated approximately 12 months prior.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:
The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax. No new or enlarging pulmonary nodules. A few subpleural micronodules in the right lower lobe measuring up to 3 mm are stable and consistent with prior granulomatous change. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. The pericardium appears unremarkable. Mediastinal and hilar contours are normal. No enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral hila, or axillae by size criteria. Visualized osseous structures are unremarkable. No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is normal in size. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesion. No biliary ductal dilatation. The surgical absence of the gallbladder is noted, consistent with prior cholecystectomy. No periportal adenopathy.

Spleen: Surgically absent, consistent with prior splenectomy. No splenosis nodules identified at the splenic bed or elsewhere in the abdomen.

Pancreas: The pancreas is unremarkable in signal and morphology. No ductal dilatation or peripancreatic inflammatory changes.

Kidneys/Adrenals: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically and demonstrate normal cortical thickness. No hydronephrosis. No suspicious renal lesions. Adrenal glands are thin and unremarkable bilaterally.

Retroperitoneum/Lymph nodes: The aorta and inferior vena cava are patent. No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. Para-aortic and paracaval nodal stations appear normal. No soft tissue masses in the retroperitoneum.

Peritoneal surfaces/Omentum: The omental resection bed is unremarkable; the residual mesentery and peritoneal surfaces appear normal without nodularity, thickening, or soft tissue implants. No evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis. No free fluid or loculated collections. No ascites.

Bowel: Post-surgical changes related to rectosigmoid resection and primary reanastomosis are again noted, with expected mucosal and mesenteric changes in the pelvis. No bowel obstruction, wall thickening, or extraluminal gas. Small and large bowel otherwise unremarkable.

PELVIS:
The uterus and bilateral adnexa are surgically absent, as expected following prior hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The vaginal cuff is unremarkable without thickening, mass, or enhancement. The bladder appears normal. No pelvic free fluid. No pelvic sidewall or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Post-surgical fibrotic changes in the pelvis are similar to prior, without new soft tissue or nodular density to suggest recurrent disease. The presacral space is clean.

BONE WINDOWS: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes in the lumbar spine are again noted, stable.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No CT evidence of recurrent or metastatic ovarian carcinoma. The chest, abdomen, and pelvis are clear.

2. Post-surgical changes are as expected: absent spleen, absent gallbladder, absence of uterus and bilateral adnexa, and rectosigmoid anastomosis — all stable and without complication.

3. No peritoneal implants, adenopathy, ascites, or parenchymal lesions to suggest disease recurrence. Overall appearance is consistent with sustained complete remission.

4. Stable subpleural micronodules in the right lower lobe, felt to represent prior granulomatous disease. No interval change.

Radiologist attestation: Reviewed and interpreted by attending radiologist.
